subject: Political Science
overall_learning_objectives: Critically evaluate the concept of power-sharing in democratic systems
Analyse the political and social consequences of majoritarianism
Compare institutional responses to ethnic diversity in Belgium and Sri Lanka
Examine prudential and moral arguments supporting power-sharing
Analyse different institutional forms of power-sharing in modern democracies
Evaluate the role of power-sharing in maintaining political stability

lesson_plan.title: Conceptual Foundations of Power-sharing
topics_covered: Definition and theoretical background of power-sharing
Concentration of power vs distributed power
student_activities: Students analyse scenarios where power concentration leads to problems

lesson_plan.title: Ethnic Diversity and Political Tensions in Belgium
Belgium’s Institutional Power-sharing Model
student_activities: Create a structural diagram of Belgium’s political system
Analyse how the system prevents conflict

lesson_plan.title: Majoritarianism and Conflict in Sri Lanka
student_activities: Prepare a comparison chart of Belgium and Sri Lanka
Discuss the political consequences of majoritarianism

lesson_plan.title: Reasons for Power-sharing
topics_covered: Power-sharing as the essence of democracy
student_activities: Students debate whether majority rule alone is sufficient in democracy
Write a short argument supporting power-sharing

lesson_plan.title: Forms of Power-sharing
teaching_strategy: Examples from global and Indian political systems
student_activities: Prepare a concept map explaining four forms of power-sharing

lesson_plan.title: Application, Reflection, and Chapter Revision
student_activities: Analyse a recent political situation involving power-sharing

teacher_tip: Encourage students to critically analyse how different political systems manage diversity, and use real-world examples of coalition governments and federal structures to deepen understanding.
